{{Short description|Change in position of an object over time}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Motion
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Classical mechanics]], [[Newton's laws of motion]], [[Force]], [[Kinematics]], [[Dynamics (mechanics)]]
}}

In [[physics]], '''motion''' is the change in position of an object with respect to its surroundings in a given interval of [[time]]. Motion is mathematically described in terms of [[displacement (geometry)|displacement]], [[distance]], [[velocity]], [[acceleration]], [[speed]], and [[time]].

== Kinematics ==
{{Main|Kinematics}}
[[Kinematics]] describes the motion of objects without reference to the forces that cause the motion. Key quantities:

* '''Position''' <math>\mathbf{r}(t)</math>
* '''Velocity''': <math>\mathbf{v} = \frac{d\mathbf{r}}{dt}</math>
* '''Acceleration''': <math>\mathbf{a} = \frac{d\mathbf{v}}{dt} = \frac{d^2\mathbf{r}}{dt^2}</math>

=== Equations of motion ===
For constant acceleration <math>a</math>:
:<math>v = v_0 + at</math>
:<math>r = r_0 + v_0 t + \frac{1}{2}at^2</math>
:<math>v^2 = v_0^2 + 2a(r - r_0)</math>

== Types of motion ==
* '''Translational motion''' — movement in a straight line or curved path
* '''Rotational motion''' — motion about an axis
* '''Periodic motion''' — repeating pattern: [[oscillation]], [[simple harmonic motion]], circular motion
* '''Projectile motion''' — motion under uniform [[gravity]]: parabolic trajectory
* '''Brownian motion''' — random motion of particles in a fluid

== Dynamics ==
{{Main|Dynamics (mechanics)}}
[[Dynamics (mechanics)|Dynamics]] connects motion to its causes — [[force]]s and [[torque]]s. [[Newton's laws of motion]] provide the fundamental framework:

# An object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by a net force ([[inertia]])
# <math>\mathbf{F} = \frac{d\mathbf{p}}{dt}</math>
# For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction

== Relativistic motion ==
At speeds approaching the [[speed of light]], classical kinematics is replaced by [[special relativity]]. The [[Lorentz transformation]] relates measurements between reference frames.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Classical mechanics]]
* [[Force]]
* [[Kinematics]]
* [[Newton's laws of motion]]
* [[Velocity]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Motion (physics)| ]]
[[Category:Classical mechanics]]
[[Category:Kinematics]]
